channel.title: ohwalker.com
channel.description: Brompton · 4 Gran Coupé · camping · travel · used-car wrenching, and everyday life

item.title: Gwanaksan Hiking Guide: Yeonjudae Summit Day Hike from Seoul
item.description: Complete guide to hiking Gwanaksan (632m) near Seoul — subway-accessible trails, Yeonjudae cliff hermitage, Palbong Ridge rock scrambles, difficulty ratings, and seasonal tips for an easy day trip.

item.title: Shokz OpenRun Pro 2 Review: Bone Conduction for Bike Commuting
item.description: A 40-something bike commuter's honest take on the orange Shokz OpenRun Pro 2: open-ear safety, lightweight neckband comfort, USB-C charging, waterproofing, and the sound-quality trade-off.

item.title: Goodbye Apple Watch Ultra, Hello Garmin Enduro 3: A Month on One Charge
item.description: After 8 months of daily charging fatigue with the Apple Watch Ultra, I switched to the Garmin Enduro 3 — readable MIP display, solar charging, and weeks of battery life.

item.title: Our Last Days in Hokkaido: When the JR Trains Stopped in Otaru
item.description: Our final days in Japan — convenience store breakfasts, Don Quijote souvenirs, and a chaotic dash from Otaru to New Chitose when every train stopped running.

item.title: Solo in Otaru: A Slow Backpacking Trip Without Snow
item.description: A relaxed, unplanned 4-night Hokkaido trip — Sapporo to Otaru's canal, century-old Airbnb house, music boxes, supermarket sushi, and gorgeous animation-like scenery.

item.title: Summer Hokkaido Day 3-4: Jozankei Onsen & Otaru Canal
item.description: A summer trip through Sapporo, Jozankei Onsen's open-air baths, and Otaru — fresh sushi, the historic canal, century-old streets, and unexpectedly hot midsummer days.

item.title: Sapporo Day Out: Ippudo Ramen and Moiwayama Observatory Views
item.description: A relaxed day in Sapporo, Hokkaido — miso ramen at Ippudo, a walk through Odori and Maruyama Park to Hokkaido Shrine, and sunset views from the Moiwayama Observatory.

item.title: Sapporo Hokkaido on a Budget: Susukino Streetcar & Ralse Market Sushi
item.description: First night in Sapporo, Hokkaido on a penny-pinching trip: riding the Susukino streetcar to Ralse market for cheap discounted sushi, highballs, and beer.

item.title: Sapporo on Foot: Beer Museum, Sapporo Factory & Odori Park
item.description: Walking Sapporo from Odori Park and the TV Tower to Sapporo Factory and the Sapporo Beer Museum — clean streets, kind locals, repurposed old buildings, and honest beer-tasting notes.

item.title: Walking to Susukino: A Foot Traveler's Sapporo Lodging Tale
item.description: Strolling through Sapporo on foot to my Susukino Airbnb—bicycle parking, pedestrian-first streets, the Clock Tower, address mix-ups, and a great-value stay.

item.title: Hokkaido Backpacking Day 1: Incheon to Sapporo by JR Rapid Airport
item.description: From DMC Station's first train to Hokkaido at 18°C — flying into New Chitose Airport, withdrawing yen via Seven Bank, riding the JR Rapid Airport to Sapporo, and a local lunch.

item.title: Sangam-dong Lamb BBQ: Genghis Khan Grilling Solo in Seoul
item.description: A Hokkaido-inspired lamb craving leads to a Sangam-dong (DMC) Genghis Khan grill. Tender Australian lamb cooked open-flame, served four delicious ways with beer.

item.title: Bike Commuting from Ilsan to Seoul Along the Han River
item.description: A 25km evening bike commute from Ilsan to Sangam-dong, Mapo-gu via the Han River path—past Haengjusanseong Fortress and World Cup Bridge in about an hour and ten.

item.title: Han River Coat Hanger Loop on a Brompton Minivelo
item.description: Riding Seoul's coat hanger course from Sangam-dong via World Cup Bridge and Jamsil Railway Bridge — 58km in 31°C heat and a brutal west wind on a 16-inch minivelo.

item.title: Brompton P Line Pedal Swap: A DIY Story After 2 Years
item.description: My 2022 Brompton P Line's stock pedals failed after two years. Here's my DIY pedal swap — the thin spanner trick and where to borrow the tool for free.

item.title: CamelBak Running Belt Silicone Water Bottle: A Real-World Review
item.description: Hands-on review of the CamelBak running belt's silicone water bottle for bike commutes, runs, and hikes—covering the double lock, fit, drinking spout, and easy daily cleaning.

item.title: MKS Sylvan Stream Pedal Upgrade for the Brompton P-line
item.description: Swapping a Brompton P-line's worn click-clacking pedals for Japan-made MKS Sylvan Stream Next pedals — fully detachable, buttery roll, and a real-world speed bump for casual riders.

item.title: Bike Commute from Ilsan Pungdong to Sangam DMC After a Week Off
item.description: Back from a week off after a degenerative disc diagnosis, I commuted by bike from Pungdong, Ilsan to Sangam-dong DMC — past Baengma crossing, Madu underpass, and Gayang Bridge.

item.title: 2022 Brompton P Line: Carbon-Rail Saddle Swap Success
item.description: Fitting a carbon-rail saddle on a Brompton P Line meant ordering the right carbon-compatible clamp. A light aftermarket part, torqued to 10nm, finally dialed it in.

item.title: Why I Bought the Brompton T Line (and Why I'd Tell You Not To)
item.description: A candid take on riding Brompton's flagship T Line, the faux-Brompton flood, why it's not worth 8.59M won, and choosing memories over owning the best.

item.title: CamelBak Ultra Belt: One Running Belt for Cycling and Jogging
item.description: Why I bought the CamelBak Ultra Belt to carry phone, keys, and a 500ml bottle while running and biking — a hands-free running belt that fits both.

item.title: Banpo to Worldcup Bridge: A Relaxed Summer Night Bike Ride
item.description: Beat the summer daytime heat with a night ride along the Hangang—Anyangcheon to Banpo Bridge's 'Banjiss' spot, Worldcup Bridge, and a comfy Mapo Bridge crossing.

item.title: Brompton T Line Tuning: Dropping Weight and Wind Drag for a Faster Ride
item.description: How stripping mudguards, lights, and extras off a stock 8.8kg Brompton T Line cut wind resistance and boosted my Han River cruising speed to 30km/h.

item.title: Brompton T Line: Removing the Quad Lock Mount, Ergon Grips, and Mudguards
item.description: Why the Quad Lock mount fails on the T Line's tapered carbon bars, plus a step-by-step guide to stripping the Ergon grips and front/rear mudguards for weight and wind savings.

item.title: 65km Round Trip to Jeongseojin on a Brompton Folding Bike
item.description: Riding a 16-inch Brompton T Line and P Line from Sangam-dong to Jeongseojin along the Ara Waterway—brutal headwinds, ocean views, planes overhead, and a refreshing 65km ride.

item.title: Brompton P Line Carbon Saddle Swap: A Clamp Snag
item.description: Swapping a carbon saddle onto a Brompton P Line hit a snag—the oval carbon rails don't fit the stock round-rail clamp. Here's what I found.

item.title: 2024 Seoul Bicycle Parade Festival: A 20km Ride Through the Heart of Seoul
item.description: My second time at the Seoul Bicycle Parade — a 20km festival ride from Gwanghwamun to World Cup Stadium on car-free roads, capped off with food at Ttukbang Super.

item.title: Sangam-dong to Han River Run: Pegasus 40 & Haneul Park
item.description: An easy early-morning run from Sangam-dong along Bulgwangcheon to the Han River in the Nike Pegasus 40, passing Worldcup Bridge and the Haneul Park metasequoia path.

item.title: Worldcup Bridge West Side to the Han River: Seoul Heart Course
item.description: A 76 km Brompton T Line ride from Sangam-dong over the Worldcup Bridge's west side, through Anyangcheon, Yangjaecheon, Gwacheon and Haguicheon back to the confluence.

item.title: My Trainer Left Mid-Package, So I'm Going Solo: Bulking to Cutting
item.description: My PT moved away with 17 sessions left, hit me with a 10% cancellation fee and a refund delay. Time to cut from 82 to 72, lace up the Alphaflys, and ride my Brompton.

item.title: Han River Night Ride on a Brompton P Line: World Cup & Banpo Bridges
item.description: A 35 km night ride from DMC across World Cup Bridge to Banpo Bridge and back on a Brompton P Line, with Han River views, the fountain show, and Yeouido's skyline.

item.title: Brompton T Line 3D Saddle Swap: AliExpress Find vs. the Original
item.description: Swapping the Brompton T Line's stock saddle for a light, breathable 3D AliExpress saddle at one-tenth the price of the Specialized original. First impressions before the ride review.

item.title: Seoul Bicycle Parade 2024: Ride Downtown Roads on May 19
item.description: Join the Seoul Bicycle Parade on Sunday, May 19, 2024, riding the city's major downtown roads. Registration opens today at 10 AM—sign up now!

item.title: Two Middle-Aged Guys Run a Lap Around Ilsan Lake Park
item.description: A first-time runner and I tackle one easy 4.7km lap around the lake park. No times, no numbers—just two 40-somethings fighting the real opponent: ourselves.

item.title: 06:00 Legs Day PT: Hip Abduction & Super Squat for a 40s Beginner
item.description: A mid-40s, one-meal-a-day beginner's dawn PT leg session — hip abduction and Panatta Super Squat machines, dialing in form, weight, and a workout-a-day routine.

item.title: Brompton T Line Flat Fix: Swapping to Schwalbe One Tires & Tubes
item.description: My Brompton T Line got a flat from a wrongly mounted tire. Here's how I swapped to Schwalbe One tires and Presta tubes, plus my Pro One upgrade plan.

item.title: Brompton T Line Garmin Mount Install: Clean Cockpit Setup
item.description: Installing a Garmin mount on the Brompton T Line's new 4-point handlebar system using an H&H universal mount—clean, no folding interference, torque-checked carbon bars.

item.title: 수색역에서 MBC까지, 신호 없이 달리는 상암동 러닝 코스
item.description: 수색역에서 출발해 수색교를 건너 MBC·DMC역까지, 신호 대기 없이 2km를 끊김 없이 달리는 추천 조깅 코스. 왕복하면 4~5km까지 늘릴 수 있습니다.

item.title: Brompton T Line Flat Tire and Torn Tube Under 50km — Factory Defect?
item.description: My Brompton T Line got a torn tire and burst tube after just 46km and a few days sitting indoors. Suspecting a factory defect, I've contacted the seller for warranty help.

item.title: Garmin Enduro 2 Review: Body Battery, Energy & Battery Life
item.description: How the Garmin Enduro 2 tracks energy, sleep recovery, and fitness with Body Battery — plus 24-day battery life and why drinking slows recovery.

item.title: Late-Night Gym Routine: A Beginner's Machine Workout
item.description: After-work weight training as a gym beginner — lat pulldown, chest press, hip adduction and abduction, plus my take on sets, rest times, and pushing for real muscle stimulus.

item.title: Sangam to DMC: An Early Morning Recovery Run
item.description: A slow, easy morning jog from MBC in Sangam-dong to DMC along the railroad-track route. Notes on pace, shoes, and scouting beginner-friendly running courses.

item.title: 2024 Brompton T Line 12-Speed: Titanium Folding Bike First Look
item.description: A first look at the 2024 Brompton T Line 12-speed — 8.8kg titanium and carbon build, PPF protection, laser-engraved decals, and ride-ready tweaks for the Han River.

item.title: Brompton T Line First Ride: Setup, Torque & Han River Break-In
item.description: Setting up the new Brompton T Line — careful 3nm/1.5nm torque, P Line grips, and a Garmin Varia 715 — then a Han River break-in ride past Yanghwa Bridge.

item.title: 40대 직장인의 새벽 공복 운동, 상암동 헬스장 등 운동 기록
item.description: 한 달 만에 다시 시작한 40대 직장인의 새벽 공복 웨이트 트레이닝. 랫풀다운, 시티드 로우, 페이스 풀로 등과 후면 어깨를 단련한 다이어트 운동 기록.

item.title: World Cup Bridge Night Ride on My Brompton T Line
item.description: An early-spring evening bike ride from Sangam-dong over World Cup Bridge to the Anyangcheon confluence and Yeouido—26km of gorgeous Han River night views on a lightweight Brompton.

item.title: Brompton Quad Lock Phone Mount: Install & Folding Test
item.description: Adding a Quad Lock phone mount with vibration damper to my Brompton. Here's the install, how it clears the fold, and the trade-offs in weight and simplicity.

item.title: Gym Beginner Confessions: 4 Months In, Just Push or Pull
item.description: A mid-40s lifting newbie shares 4 months of gym lessons: forget fancy gear and heavy weights — just show up, push or pull, and your body starts to change.

item.title: Garmin Enduro 2 Watch Bands: Titanium, Silicone & Nylon
item.description: My months with the Garmin Enduro 2 and the bands I rotate—titanium for the office, silicone for all-weather use, and lightweight nylon for daily wear and running.

item.title: Brompton Noodle Run to Ddukbang Super: A Spring-ish Bike Ride
item.description: A casual 31 km Brompton ride to Ddukbang Super for crispy kimchi-jeon, dubu-kimchi, and umami janchi-guksu—soaking up nostalgic country charm and the first hint of spring.

item.title: Building a Workout Habit: A Beginner's Gym Journey
item.description: How I finally made exercise a habit—gym routine, Versa Gripps for weak forearms, moderate weights, and training environment tips for lifelong fitness in your 40s.

item.title: A Heavy-Snow Day Crash That Pushed Me Toward a Lighter Life
item.description: A snowy collision on Namsan totaled my Sienna and left me hospitalized — but it became the excuse to declutter, downsize to an old car, and rethink what truly matters.

item.title: 40대 다이어트 3주 만에 체지방 4% 감량한 점심시간 운동 루틴
item.description: 체지방 26%에서 22%로, 173cm 81kg 40대 직장인의 지속 가능한 운동 습관 만들기. 점심시간 헬스, 닭가슴살 식단, 프리웨이트로 평생 갈 루틴을 완성한 실제 후기.

item.title: A Quiet Day Off: An IT Leader's Pause to Breathe
item.description: An IT PM's 12-hour days demand downtime. A trip to Soraepogu fish market, raw fish, and a market walk — captured on Leica M11 and iPhone 15 Pro.

item.title: Versa Gripps Pro Review: Why I Upsized from S to L
item.description: Two weeks into weight training, I swapped my too-short Versa Gripps Pro for the larger 2023 model with a wider velcro strap — here's why thicker wrists need it.

item.title: Nike Metcon 8 Review: Why I Switched From Soft Running Shoes for Lifting
item.description: After turning 40 and overhauling my health, my PT said my shoes were too soft. Here's my honest take on the Nike Metcon 8 for CrossFit, weights, and treadmill cardio.

item.title: Garmin Enduro 2 Guided Run: 4km at a 6:55 Pace
item.description: A casual evening run with the Garmin Enduro 2's workout suggestion—4km in 27 minutes, 300+ kcal burned, plus protein-focused meals and cold-weather running tips.

item.title: Nike Alphafly Racing Shoes: A Middle-Aged Runner's Honest Take
item.description: My take on the Nike Alphafly 1 — carbon FLYPLATE, half-length Air Zoom units, sky-high cushioning. Why splurging on high-end running gear is worth it for fewer injuries.

item.title: From Tesla Model Y to the Toyota Sienna: Why I Switched
item.description: After years in a Tesla Model Y, I traded its harsh ride for the Toyota Sienna — a roomy, fuel-sipping minivan built for car camping, travel, and photography.

item.title: Nike Pegasus 40 Review: A Neutral Running Shoe for Coming Back After 40
item.description: Back pain was the real culprit behind my knee and ankle aches. Easing into running again, I tried the Nike Pegasus 40 — breathable, durable, and comfy at 83kg.

item.title: Autumn Riding Along the Han River Before Winter Sets In
item.description: After a brutal summer, cool autumn arrives. A long ride along the Han River past Banpo and Sangam — battling fierce headwinds all the way back to Ilsan.

item.title: Jirisan Seongjung Traverse: Lessons From a Failed Single-Day Attempt
item.description: A single-day Seongsamjae–Jungsanri traverse of Jirisan that didn't go to plan—gear failures, energy crashes, training gaps, and hard-won advice for anyone attempting it.

item.title: Garmin Varia 715 Radar Dash Cam: First Look Before My Cross-Country Ride
item.description: Unboxing the Garmin Varia 715 radar dash cam for safety on cross-country cycling. First impressions on size, pairing, battery life, and charging tips.

item.title: Garmin Edge 840 Solar Review: A Bike Computer on a Brompton P Line
item.description: Why I chose the Garmin Edge 840 Solar for my Brompton minivelo—solar charging, USB-C, sensors, and why tracking ride data is worth it even on a folding bike.

item.title: Brompton Grip Swap: Installing Ergon GS2 Grips in a Parking Lot
item.description: Swapping a Brompton's stock grips for Ergon GS2 grips using only the built-in Brompton Toolkit, plus tips on S-bar fit, torque, and a relaxed Han River ride.

item.title: HJC Atara Aero Helmet Review: Lightweight, Cool Pick for Brompton Riders
item.description: My honest take on the HJC Atara aero helmet — at ~200g it's far lighter, cooler, and better vented than my urban helmet, and ideal for summer Brompton rides.

item.title: Five Ten Freerider Review: Flat Pedal Shoes for My Brompton
item.description: After swapping in MKS pedals, I needed proper flat-pedal shoes for my Brompton. Here's my take on the Five Ten Freerider after 30 km of riding.

item.title: Brompton Grip Upgrade: Installing Ergon GS2 Grips for Long Rides
item.description: Swapped my Brompton's stock rubber grips for Ergon GS2 grips. Here's how they ease palm and wrist fatigue with three hand positions on rides over an hour.

item.title: Brompton P Line in Emerald Green: My Fitness & Touring Setup
item.description: A hands-on look at my Brompton P Line in Emerald Green — 21T gear swap, low S bar, Specialized saddle, front bag, and a 10.4kg build for cross-country touring.

item.title: Cycling the Han River: Ilsan to Mapo on My Brompton
item.description: Swapping running for cycling, I took an easy 47km round trip from Ilsan to Mapo along the Han River — muddy water, washed-up trash, but a gorgeous night view.

item.title: Susaek Station Lunch Walk: An Old Seoul Neighborhood by the Underpass
item.description: A leisurely 2km lunchtime stroll from Susaek Station through an underpass to DMC, exploring an aged Seoul neighborhood full of old-world charm and quiet rainy-day scenes.

item.title: Therm-a-Rest Uberlite Regular Review: A 10-Year Summer Air Mat
item.description: After 10 years I replaced my air mat with a Therm-a-Rest Uberlite Regular. Light, packs under 1L, comfy full-length—my honest review vs the Nemo Tensor Short.

item.title: Gossamer Gear Fast Kumo: A Light, Pretty Summer Backpacking Pack
item.description: My honest take on the 610g Gossamer Gear Fast Kumo — why I chose it for summer trips, the overseas ordering and customs hassle, and how it compares to my Lightning 45.

item.title: 철원 DMZ 국제마라톤 하프코스 도전, 부상 후 러닝 복귀기
item.description: 발목 부상으로 쉬었던 러닝을 다시 시작하며 철원 DMZ 국제마라톤 하프코스에 도전합니다. 5:30 페이스로 2시간 내 완주를 목표로 고석정에서 달립니다.

item.title: Noeul Camping Ground: Kolon Aerolite 2 Backpacking Review
item.description: Snagging a cancellation at Seoul's Noeul Camping Ground for a humid, mosquito-filled night — plus a hands-on review of the green Kolon Aerolite 2 tent.

item.title: Kolon Aerolight 2 Unboxing: A 1.7kg Freestanding Tent Review
item.description: Hands-on unboxing of Kolon's Aerolight 2, a sub-1.7kg double-wall freestanding two-person backpacking tent. Solid build, half the price of Hilleberg, but missing stake pull strings.

item.title: Wildo Camping Cookware Set Review: 265g, Stacks 9 Pieces in One
item.description: A hands-on look at Wildo's Swedish 265g camping set—nesting cups, dual bowls, cutting board, seasoning box, and a serrated spork—built for solo trips and backpacking.

item.title: Camping Gear Care: My Post-Trip Routine to Make Equipment Last
item.description: Hot summer means peak camping season. Here's my simple post-trip routine—same-day cookware cleaning and a fast circulator method to dry tents, sleeping bags, and dishes.

item.title: 파평산 백패킹: 서울 근교 초보 추천 코스와 주차장 가이드
item.description: 파주 파평산 백패킹 후기. 서울 근교 50분 거리, 무료 주차장과 완만/급경사 코스, 일출·일몰 명당과 데크 야영지까지 초보자에게 추천하는 낮은 산 정보를 담았습니다.

item.title: Garmin Forerunner 955 Review: Best-Value Sports Watch for Everyday Athletes
item.description: A hands-on gift review of the Garmin Forerunner 955: up to 15-day battery, solid water resistance, reliable notifications, and top Forerunner features at under half the price of Fenix.

item.title: Resizing a Used Titanium Garmin Watch Band with the Right Tool
item.description: I grabbed a used titanium Garmin band on Karrot, but it was too small. Here's how I resized the tube-link band myself with a cheap kit for a perfect fit.

item.title: Why I Left the Apple Watch Ultra and Went Back to Garmin
item.description: After 4 years on Apple Watch, battery anxiety, weak pace display, and paid apps drove me back to Garmin. My honest take on sports watch vs smartwatch.

item.title: Unboxing the Dyneema Moment LI DW: My Overseas Splurge
item.description: A hands-on unboxing of the Dyneema Moment LI DW ultralight tent ordered from the US—carbon poles, Cuben fiber fly, packed size vs. Hilleberg Enan, and first impressions.

item.title: Tarptent Moment DW Li: My Ultralight Dyneema Tent for Backpacking Again
item.description: A 745g Dyneema single-pole tent for the UL world. Why I chose the Moment Li with mesh interior and vestibule to backpack lighter and safer after a long break.

item.title: Jetboil Stash Review: A 200g Ultralight Cookset for Backpacking
item.description: My hands-on take on the Jetboil Stash: a 200g titanium-and-aluminum cookset that runs on a 110g iso canister, with a built-in silicone handle and nesting design for ultralight trips.

item.title: Hilleberg Enan: My Sub-1kg One-Person Tent for a Backpacking Return
item.description: After an ankle injury, I'm restarting backpacking with the Hilleberg Enan — a lightweight one-person tent under 1kg with a roomy vestibule and all-weather build.

item.title: Quiet Days After Quitting: Camera, Trotters, and a Glass of Wine in Sangam-dong
item.description: Three months after leaving my job, sidelined by ankle tendonitis, I picked the camera back up — pig's trotters, wine, and Giant Olive beer in Sangam-dong.

item.title: Walking Down the Street: Spring Azaleas Shot on Leica M11
item.description: A spring walk paused for pretty flowers, captured on a Leica M11 with a Voigtlander Nokton 40mm, plus thoughts on weight loss and easing ankle pain in my 40s.

item.title: Lunchtime Walks and Street Photography in Sangam-dong
item.description: A developer working in Sangam-dong shares lunchtime walks and photos shot on a Leica M11 with a Summilux 35mm f1.4, capturing early spring in March 2023.

item.title: A Lunchtime Walk Along the Sangam-dong Stream
item.description: On a lunch break in Sangam-dong, I walked a quiet stream path with a Bamseom island and open fields—still wintry, but greenery is coming soon.

item.title: Solo Hiking Cookware: Snow Peak Titanium vs Kovea Alpine Master
item.description: A look at two backpacking cooksets for multi-day hikes—Snow Peak's light titanium combo and the wind-beating, ramen-friendly Kovea Alpine Master (Coalma).

item.title: ITRA National League 2023 Korea: Trail Running Guide
item.description: A rundown of Korea's ITRA National League 2023 trail run on April 29, with 50K, 22K, and 10K courses from Dongducheon — distances, elevation, cutoffs, ITRA points, and fees.

item.title: Why I Joined ITRA for Trail Running Season
item.description: I joined the International Trail Running Association (ITRA) to track race schedules like Seoul 100K and Sahara 250K and prepare more systematically for trail running.

item.title: 10 Benefits of Trail Running: Why You Should Hit the Trails
item.description: Trail running boosts fitness, balance, cardio endurance, and focus while easing stress and connecting you with nature. Here are 10 benefits of trail running.

item.title: Apple Watch Ultra Review: Battery, GPS & Hiking for iPhone Users
item.description: Hands-on Apple Watch Ultra review covering its big battery boost, dual-band GPS accuracy, rugged design, fast charging, and whether it works for hiking, trekking, and outdoor sports.

item.title: Garmin Fenix 7X Sapphire Solar Bands: AliExpress vs Genuine
item.description: Comparing Garmin Fenix 7X Sapphire Solar watch bands — genuine leather and silicone vs cheap 26mm QuickFit AliExpress straps, including an Alpine Loop copy.

item.title: Gwanaksan Full Ridge Traverse: 11 Flag Peaks, 20km Course from Sadang Station
item.description: A complete guide to the 19.54km Gwanaksan 11-flag-peak ridge traverse from Sadang Station to Gwanaksan Station — timing, water, food, and safety tips for the 14-hour route.

item.title: Bukhansan Dulle-gil: Planning a Two-Day Trek Near Seoul
item.description: A full overview of Bukhansan Dulle-gil's 71.5 km loop and 21 themed sections, with route planning, timing, resupply tips, and a west-start two-day, one-night trekking strategy.

item.title: Hiking Simhak Mountain in Paju: A Beginner-Friendly Trail with Parking
item.description: A 41-minute, 2km round-trip hike up Paju's Simhak Mountain. Free parking at Yakcheonsa Temple, a ridge climb, Peace Nuri Trail views, and tips for beginners.

item.title: How to Use Trekking Poles the Right Way: Strap Grip & Technique
item.description: Learn the correct way to use trekking poles — hold the strap, not the grip. Cut joint strain, reduce leg fatigue, and shift up to 40% of the load to your poles.

item.title: Hiking Jirisan's Hwa-Dae Traverse: A 46.2 km Section-by-Section Guide
item.description: A detailed breakdown of Jirisan's 46.2 km Hwaeomsa-to-Daewonsa traverse—every section from Nogodan to Cheonwangbong, plus single-day trail-running tips and a GPX.

item.title: Seoul 100K Ultra-Trail: My 2023 Trail Running Goal
item.description: Exploring the Seoul 100K and 50K ultra-trail courses through Inwangsan, Bukhansan, and downtown Seoul — and my plan to finish a trail running race in 2023.

item.title: How to Start Hiking: 6 Beginner Tips to Lose the Fear
item.description: Hiking isn't hard or expensive. Here are 6 simple tips for beginners—start with low mountains, wear sneakers and workout clothes, and pack snacks—to start enjoying it.

item.title: Pairing a Garmin Fenix 7X With a New iPhone: What Finally Worked
item.description: After two days of failed syncs, here's the step-by-step fix to properly pair a Garmin Fenix 7X with a new iPhone 14 Pro and restore full Garmin Connect data.

item.title: Suunto 9 Peak vs Garmin Fenix 7X: Sports Watch Comparison
item.description: A hands-on comparison of the Suunto 9 Peak and Garmin Fenix 7X — design, build, battery, GPS performance, and how a dedicated sports watch differs from a smartwatch.

item.title: Hiking & Running Ankle Ligament Injuries: Treatment and Recovery Tips
item.description: My experience with a grade 1 ankle sprain from running—why it happens regardless of age, how braces and pain patches help, and why early treatment and patience matter.

item.title: Why Hiking Is the Ultimate Full-Body Workout
item.description: From burning 4,000+ kcal on Jirisan to forest bathing and stress relief, here's how hiking delivers cardio, weight loss, healing, and a real sense of success.

item.title: My Hiking First-Aid Kit Essentials: Meds, Patches & a Survival Blanket
item.description: A look inside my lightweight 200–300g hiking first-aid kit: painkillers, digestive aid, bandages, coin pain patches, liquid gel, and a survival blanket that can save lives.

item.title: Sobaeksan Jukgu Traverse: A Brutal Autumn Day Hike from Jungnyeong to Guinsa
item.description: Hiking the 20km+ Sobaeksan Jukgu Traverse from Jungnyeong Pass to Guinsa Temple—brutal Baekdudaegan winds, freezing autumn cold, and breathtaking ridgeline views worth the climb.

item.title: Arc'teryx Alpha SV Review: The Flagship Gore-Tex Hardshell
item.description: A hands-on look at the Arc'teryx Alpha SV hardshell — chest pockets, pit-zip ventilation, storm hood, fit and sizing tips for serious hiking.

item.title: Oseosan: Highest Peak in the Geumbuk Range & Backpacking Guide
item.description: Hike Oseosan (790m), the West Sea's lighthouse, via the Sangdam parking lot loop. Spacious deck sites for backpacking and famous autumn silver grass.

item.title: Shokz OpenRun Pro Bone-Conduction Earphones: A 1-Year Review
item.description: My honest year-long review of the Shokz OpenRun Pro bone-conduction Bluetooth earphones for running and hiking—open-ear design, 8-hour battery, fast charging, and sweat resistance.

item.title: Brynje Super Thermo Base Layer: Why Mesh Keeps You Warm
item.description: After a winter hiking break, I picked up two Brynje Super Thermo mesh base layers. Here's how the mesh traps air for warmth while staying sweat-free and comfortable.

item.title: 잠발란 토파네 8-9년 등산화 리뷰 — 국산 vs 수입 등산화 진실
item.description: 10년 산행, 10여 켤레 끝에 정착한 잠발란 토파네. 사이즈·미끄럼·내구성에 대한 오해를 짚고, 노르웨이지언 웰트와 8년 사용 후기를 솔직하게 풀어낸 풀그레인 가죽 등산화 리뷰.

item.title: Nike Alphafly 1 Review: A 40s Beginner's First Running Shoes
item.description: A paunchy 40-something's honest take on the Nike Alphafly 1 — design, carbon plate, ZoomX foam, training, and finishing a rainy 10K in his first marathon shoes.

item.title: Nogosan: An Easy Loop Hike Near Seoul With Big Views
item.description: Nogosan is a beginner-friendly two-hour loop trail near Seoul with free parking at Heungguksa, stunning Bukhansan views, and a downloadable GPX track.

item.title: 서울근교산행 북한산 — Bukhansan National Park Hiking Guide
item.description: Bukhansan, Seoul's closest national park, offers 14+ rocky trails. Bring plenty of water, snacks, and a charged phone—it's close, but never an easy climb.

item.title: Minivelo & Brompton: A Folding Bike Lifestyle Guide
item.description: Discover the freedom of minivelo and Brompton folding bikes—extend your range via subway, bus, and air travel, stay healthy, and weave cycling into daily life.

item.title: Free E-book Giveaway: My Journey to 40 Million Won in Blog Income
item.description: 쭌이덕 shares a free 40-page blogging e-book covering AdPost earnings, mutual neighbors, and monetization tips. Apply Dec 21–Jan 1—first come, first served.

item.title: Mudeungsan National Park: A Scenic Loop Traverse Hiking Guide
item.description: Hike Mudeungsan, Gwangju's guardian mountain, on a 16km loop past Gyubongam and Seoseokdae's columnar joints. Well-kept trails, water refills, and stunning views.

item.title: Hiking Gear Essentials: A Beginner's Guide to Summer, Winter & Backpacking
item.description: A practical rundown of hiking gear for beginners — seasonal clothing, backpack must-haves like headlamps and trail food, plus backpacking tent and sleeping setup tips.

item.title: Black Diamond Distance 15 Vest Pack: A Hiker's Honest Review
item.description: A hands-on review of the Black Diamond Distance 15 vest-style hiking pack — its pockets, pole holders, fit, and why packing light beats packing heavy on the trail.

item.title: Trail Running Shoes: Lightweight Hiking Gear for Three Seasons
item.description: A hands-on look at trail running shoes—light, breathable, Vibram-gripped hiking footwear. How they differ from mid-cut boots and tips for protecting weaker ankles.

item.title: Jirisan Traverse Passbook: Collecting Shelter Stamps from Hwaeomsa to Cheonwangbong
item.description: A Jirisan traverse trip from Hwaeomsa to Jungsan-ri—collecting shelter stamps at Nogodan, Yeonhacheon, Seseok, and Jangteomok, plus GPX data and travel tips.

item.title: Caring for 7-Year-Old Zamberlan Tofane Leather Hiking Boots
item.description: How I clean and condition my seven-year-old Zamberlan Tofane full-grain leather hiking boots with Saphir leather balm and a hog bristle brush to make them last.

item.title: Off-Season Road Bike Check-Up: TREK Emonda SL7 Maintenance
item.description: An off-season TREK Emonda SL7 check-up: cleaning carbon tubeless wheels, oiling the chain, testing Di2 and Ultegra shifting, and prepping the bike before the new riding season.

item.title: Garmin Smartwatch Battery Life and Charging Tips
item.description: Garmin smartwatches last about two weeks per charge. Here's my real-world charging routine, plus a USB-C adapter trick for the non-standard 4-pin cable.

item.title: Winter Bike Care: Storing My Trek Émonda and Brompton
item.description: How I keep my DI2 road bike and Brompton in shape through winter — battery top-ups, idle spins, tire pressure, and a little chain oil until spring rides return.

item.title: Mounting a GoPro on My Brompton with Rixen & Kaul (Plus a Kickstand)
item.description: My GoPro mounting saga continues: trying a German Rixen & Kaul mount on my Brompton M-bar, admitting Trigo was fine, and adding a kickstand—still no perfect cockpit setup.

item.title: Installing Omni Wheels on My Brompton for Easier Rolling
item.description: I swapped my Brompton's stock easy wheels for bearing-equipped omni wheels for smoother side-to-side handling — plus a tolerance defect and surprisingly good Chinese after-sales service.

item.title: Anguk to Ilsan by Brompton: A 48km Bike Commute Through Seoul
item.description: A relaxed 48km Brompton ride from Anguk Station through Namsan, Itaewon, and Mapo to Ilsan—3 hours 41 minutes of safe cycling, dinner with a friend, and a workout commute home.

item.title: Adding an AliExpress Dashcam to a Brompton P Line Commuter
item.description: I mounted a cheap AliExpress dashcam on my friend's Brompton commuter. Easy GoPro-style fit, but the shaky footage without stabilization shows its limits. One-month update coming.

item.title: Brompton Grip Swap to Ergon: A Mapo Ride and DIY Surgery
item.description: A Han River ride out to Mapo on a hub dynamo Brompton, then swapping the stock grips for Ergon grips at home—cutting them to fit, the mistakes, and the comfy result.

item.title: Apple Watch Ultra vs Garmin Fenix 7X: Hands-On Comparison
item.description: A real-world comparison of the Apple Watch Ultra and Garmin Fenix 7X—battery life, sports tracking, screen readability, and which smartwatch fits you best.

item.title: Brompton Seatpost Insert: Easy DIY Seat-Height Memory Install
item.description: Installing a Brompton seatpost insert gives you a fixed seat-height memory for fast, repeatable folding. A simple hex-wrench DIY anyone can do, plus P Line dynamo light details.

item.title: Brompton Roll-Top Bag Review: 28L Laptop Bag for Commuting
item.description: A hands-on look at Brompton's large roll-top bag — fits a 16-inch MacBook Pro, loads of external pockets, included rain cover, and YKK zippers for bike commuting.

item.title: Brompton P Line with Hub Dynamo: My New Build at bb5 Seobinggo
item.description: Picking up a P Line with rear rack, hub dynamo, Edelux headlight, and SON taillight at bb5 Seobinggo — plus rolling resistance notes and a dak-galbi dinner.

item.title: Garmin Fenix 7X vs Apple Watch Ultra: The Sports Watch Battle
item.description: Comparing two flagship sports watches — Garmin Fenix 7X and Apple Watch Ultra — on battery life, GPS accuracy, build quality, and real-world outdoor use.

item.title: A Full Day of Brompton Riding: Noodles, Coffee Runs, and Han River Eats
item.description: An 80km Brompton day from Haengju Fortress to Ilsan and back — chogye guksu, savory yukjeon, a spill-prone coffee run, GoPro mount woes, and Han River ramyeon.

item.title: Charging Up for a Night Ride: Lights, Di2 & Garmin Prep
item.description: Prepping for my first night road bike ride in ages — charging headlight, tail light, Shimano Di2, and Garmin, plus a new skull cap and a windbreaker.

item.title: Brompton P-line Subway Commute: Folding Bike Tips for Rush Hour
item.description: My Brompton P-line subway commute from Madu to Anguk Station — folding the bike, dodging rush-hour crowds, train gaps, and avoiding chain grease on packed cars.

item.title: Brompton Ride Along the Ara Waterway to Jeongseojin
item.description: A holiday afternoon Brompton ride from Haengju Bridge along the Ara Waterway to Jeongseojin — 45km round trip, a so-so jjamppong lunch, and a stunning sunset at magic hour.

item.title: Why I Ditched My Fizik Saddle for a Specialized One
item.description: My Fizik saddle put all the pressure in the wrong place. Here's why I swapped to a comfortable Specialized saddle that works for both me and my wife.

item.title: 80km on the Brompton: New Gearing and a Lost Pair of Goggles
item.description: An 80km Brompton ride through Yeouido to Ilsan — adding a 21T cog, thoughts on professionalism, riding home after a downpour, and losing my 10-year-old goggles.

item.title: Hoka Running Cap Review & Night Run in Alphafly at Byeolmuri Stadium
item.description: I bought a Hoka running cap — great finish and fast-drying. Ran 10 easy laps in the Alphafly 1 and Injinji socks while nursing my ankle, prepping for trail running next year.

item.title: Brompton 4-Speed Stock Specs Before My Lightweight Tuning Project
item.description: Recording the stock specs of my 4-speed Brompton (9.89kg) before tuning: removing mudguards, swapping the cassette to 21T, lighter bottle, and a possible saddle change for safer weight savings.

item.title: DIY Brompton P Line Weight-Saving: Removing Mudguards & Rear Rack
item.description: I DIY'd the first round of weight-saving on my Brompton P Line—pulling the front and rear mudguards, rear rack, and reflector. Easier than expected, with a few quirks to plan for.

item.title: Brompton P Line Night Ride to Han River Ramen Spot
item.description: A relaxed night ride to the Ministop Gangseo Branch #1 for Han River ramen — two Brompton P Lines, 32 km, 19 km/h average, and an easy 2h42m of riding from Ilsan.

item.title: Brompton P Line Gear-Shifting Trouble: How I Diagnosed and Fixed It
item.description: My Brompton P Line lost 1st and 2nd gear, so I adjusted the cable tension myself, hit a parts-shortage wall, and got a temporary fix to keep riding.

item.title: Finding a Sold-Out Billingham Game Bag in Olive for My Brompton
item.description: My taste runs classic and long-lasting. After years with a Billingham Hadley Pro, I tracked down a rare unused olive Brompton game bag — now I just need a frame.

item.title: Road Bike vs. Mini Velo: Trek Emonda or Brompton P-Line?
item.description: Owning both a Trek Emonda SL7 road bike and a Brompton P-Line mini velo, here's the real-world trade-off between speed and convenience — and why I need to choose.

item.title: Late-Autumn Han River Night Ride: Emonda SL7 & Brompton P Line
item.description: A chilly night ride along the Han River with my road bike and Brompton, capped off with open-air ramen. Squeezing in rides before the cold sets in.

item.title: Going Light: My Trail Running Switch from Heavy Hiking Gear
item.description: Why I ditched mid-cut boots and a heavy pack for a vest-style 15L pack, soft flasks, and Hoka Tecton X shoes — and how the lighter setup handled 800m+ climbs.

item.title: My Bike Headlight Stopped Charging: A Proprietary 18650 Battery Headache
item.description: My Type-C headlight charges but won't light up. The proprietary 18650 battery only charges in-unit, making failures a real risk on night rides. Check yours often.

item.title: Garmin Fenix 7X vs Apple Watch: Why Battery Life Won
item.description: After years on an Apple Watch, I switched to the Garmin Fenix 7X for running and long hikes. Here's how the data and 21-day battery changed my training.

item.title: Buying a Brompton P Line: An 800 km Round Trip to Gwangju
item.description: After endless searching, I drove 800 km to Gwangju to buy two rare Brompton P Lines, then rode the Buan coast. A story of choices and focus.

item.title: My Brompton's Brutal Initiation: A Fall Bike Ride Gone Sideways
item.description: A weekend ride with my kid took a turn when my new Brompton rolled through animal droppings. Here's how I cleaned, dried, and still fell for this fold-up bike.

item.title: Thousand Helmet Review: The Perfect Match for Your Brompton
item.description: A hands-on review of the new Thousand helmet with MIPS, magnetic buckle, signature lock hole, and a free tail light — and why it pairs perfectly with a Brompton.

item.title: Are You Wearing Your Bike Helmet Wrong? Quick Fit Check
item.description: Many riders wear helmets incorrectly. The chin straps near your ears should drop straight down vertically. Wear it right and ride safe.

item.title: Under an Hour: My First Sub-60 10K at a 6:00 Pace
item.description: After an awkward post-dinner hour, a planned 5K turned into a 10K. I pushed through stomach trouble and tired ankles to finish just under an hour—by 11 seconds.

item.title: Going Tubeless: Converting My Bontrager Carbon Wheels
item.description: My first tubeless conversion — why bike tires flat, how sealant replaces the tube, sealing carbon wheels with tape and a valve, plus learning basic wheel removal.

item.title: Hoka Tecton Run: A Sub-5 Pace Through Autumn Streets
item.description: Back home after two days at a hotel, I ran a hilly 4.5km neighborhood loop in my Hoka Tectons, holding a sub-5-minute pace nearly the whole way.

item.title: Nike Pushup Bars for Home Training: My Last Gym Year
item.description: Done with gym memberships, I switched to pushup bars for home upper-body workouts. After several cheap busts, these are easiest on the hands with a longer range of motion.

item.title: Banyan Tree Hotel: A Place to Completely Switch Off
item.description: Sometimes you just need to completely switch off. Discover the serene escape of Banyan Tree Hotel — a retreat designed for total rest and quiet relaxation.

item.title: Kovea Alpine Master: A Korean Reactor That Rivals MSR
item.description: After 10+ years with the MSR Reactor, the Korean-made Kovea Alpine Master stacks cookset, burner, and gas into one neat package — boils two packs of ramen with ease.

item.title: Treadmill Lactate Threshold Intervals with My Garmin Fenix 7X
item.description: With a banged-up ankle, I hit the gym for treadmill interval running tracked on my Garmin Fenix 7X — short recovery, a nice graph, and chicken instead of beer.

item.title: First Run with My New Vest Pack on Bukhansan
item.description: Tteurak tests a vest-style hydration pack on Bukhansan — two 600ml bottles, gimbap, trail snacks, and 2L water. Snug, low-bounce, surprisingly free.

item.title: Trail Running and Hiking Gear: My Ten-Year Journey to Lighter Adventures
item.description: Tteurak shares a decade-long path from backpacking to hiking to trail running, plus the lightweight gear—packs, gloves, carabiner, and softshell—that makes running free.

item.title: HOKA Tecton X Trail Review: First Run on Jirisan
item.description: Testing HOKA's Tecton X on a Jirisan traverse — faster than hiking boots, with a breathable upper, wide toe box, and great grip, but less ankle support.

item.title: Conquering Seoraksan's Gongnyong Ridge: Korea's Toughest Hike
item.description: Tackling Gongnyong Ridge with a bad ankle—predawn climbs, sunrise views, King Kong Rock, and a hard-won stamp at Huiungak Shelter on Korea's most grueling course.

item.title: Why I Made Sleep My Top Priority This Year
item.description: This year I focused on better sleep — asleep before 11, nearly 8 hours in bed, 7 hours of rest. Here's how my days keep starting earlier.

item.title: Prepping for Seoraksan's Dinosaur Ridge: My Full Gear Checklist
item.description: Tteurak gears up for Seoraksan's Dinosaur Ridge—water, snacks, headlamp, hardshell, Black Diamond gloves, Leki poles, and a Klättermusen pack. Here's everything I packed.

item.title: Ankle Ligament Injury From Running: My Clinic Visit
item.description: Hurt my ankle running and saw the doctor — turns out the ligament's inflamed. No running while it heals, plus pricey uninsured pain clinic visits. Lessons on self-care.

item.title: Cheonggyecheon Barefoot: A Restful Day Before Chuseok
item.description: The day before Chuseok, I shopped at Arc'teryx in Jongno 5-ga, ate at Gwangjang Market, and dipped my feet in the cool Cheonggyecheon stream with a friend.

item.title: Road Bike Clipless Pedals: Worth It or Time to Ditch Them?
item.description: My honest take on riding clipless pedals on a road bike — efficient and great for long distances, but one clip-in fall can wreck your knee. Here's my call.

item.title: Half Marathon Prep: My Messy First Track Interval Run
item.description: Training for a half marathon with interval runs at Byeolmuri Track. Pacing chaos, watch lag, and why the Hoka Tecton shines on the track too.

item.title: New USB-C Bike Taillight: 100 Lumens, All-Season Battery Life
item.description: Swapped my old bike's taillight for a USB-C rechargeable 100-lumen one—bright enough for regular roads and built to last a full season without battery worries.

item.title: Free-Admission Palace Walk: Changdeokgung to Changgyeonggung Loop
item.description: On the month's last Wednesday, palaces are free. A 3.7km loop walk from Changdeokgung to Changgyeonggung after lunch — a lovely course for a light sweat.

item.title: Hiking Jokduri Peak With My Son
item.description: A father-and-son hike up Jokduri Peak. He's a remarkably strong hiker for his age, and I'm simply grateful to watch him keep growing up healthy.

item.title: Cheorwon DMZ Marathon: My First Race in the Rain
item.description: Running my first marathon in the rain from Typhoon Hinnamnor. Despite a left ankle injury, I held a steady 6:30 pace and finished healthy and grateful.

item.title: My First Treadmill Run: Half-Marathon Interval Training
item.description: Typhoon Hinnamnor pushed my run indoors. I tackled 12/9 km/h intervals for 30 minutes, covering just over 5 km on the treadmill. Here's how tough it felt.

item.title: My First International Race: The Cheorwon DMZ 10K Marathon
item.description: I ran my first-ever international race at the Cheorwon DMZ Marathon. It rained the whole 10K, but it was one of the best, most meaningful days I've had in ages.

item.title: Jirisan Seongjung Traverse: 37.5km in Arc'teryx & Hoka
item.description: A 37.5km Jirisan Seongjung traverse in light rain under 24°C, with gear notes on the Arc'teryx Gamma LT, Hoka Tecton, and Injinji double-layer socks.

item.title: Time to Lace Up: Getting Back Into Running After Work
item.description: I need to get more exercise and make time for runs after work. It's even better running alongside people who make me smile. #Alphafly #Running
